items.q: When are you available to start?
items.a: I usually have one or two slots opening per quarter. Book a discovery call and I'll tell you the soonest realistic start date.

items.q: Do you work with international clients?
items.a: Yes — I'm in Dubai (GMT+4) but work with founders from Europe, the US, and across MENA. Async-friendly and on-call when needed.

items.q: What if my project changes mid-build?
items.a: It usually does. We track changes weekly. Small ones are absorbed; large ones get a quick re-scope so you stay in control of budget and timeline.

items.q: Can you refer me if you're not the right fit?
items.a: Absolutely. I have a small network of trusted designers and engineers I refer to. No commission — just trying to help you ship.

processPage.metaDesc: Six clearly-defined stages from first call to post-launch support. Transparent, fixed-price, weekly demos.
processPage.ticker: PROCESS · DISCOVERY → PROPOSAL → DESIGN → SPRINTS → LAUNCH → SUPPORT | WEEKLY DEMOS | YOUR CODE · YOUR CLOUD

header.desc: No surprises, no vague promises. Six clearly-defined stages from the first call to post-launch support. You see real progress every week, and you're always in control — of the scope, the schedule, and the code.

stages.p: A 30-minute conversation to understand your goals, constraints, and what "done" actually looks like. I ask the tough questions upfront so we avoid expensive surprises later.
li: What problem does this solve, and for whom?
What's your budget, and is it realistic for the scope?
What does success look like — in metrics, not adjectives?

stages.p: Within 48 hours of our call, you get a document — stack recommendation, timeline, itemized budget, and concrete deliverables. No vague promises, everything specific and measurable.
li: Line-item budget with no hidden fees

stages.p: Wireframes, database schema, API contracts, UI mockups — everything documented before a single line of production code is written. You approve every step.
A live demo every Friday. You see real progress, give real feedback, and we adjust before small changes become expensive. Your staging environment is always reachable.
li: Daily commits visible in your repo

stages.p: Full QA pass, performance testing, security review, and deployment on your infrastructure — your servers or your cloud, not mine. Complete handover with written documentation.
30 days of free bug fixes — you shouldn't pay to fix my mistakes. Optional monthly retainer afterwards for ongoing features, security patches, and improvements.
li: Clear escalation path for urgent issues

items: You see the real state of the work every week, even the messy bits. Polish comes at the end; visibility comes from day one.
Every line belongs to you, 100%. Deployed on your cloud, your servers, your domain. No vendor lock-in, ever.
The price you sign is the price you pay. If scope changes, we renegotiate openly — no silent overruns, no billed surprises.
Grand visions only survive on solid foundations. We agree the schema, contracts and flows before the first commit.
If I don't know, I'll say so. If a deadline is at risk, you'll hear it early — not the day it slips.
Every feature ships with a performance budget. Slow software is broken software, even when all the tests pass.

faqPage.metaDesc: Frequently asked questions about working together — availability, pricing, payment, IP, and support.
faqPage.ticker: FAQ · ANSWERS BEFORE YOU ASK | AVAILABILITY · PRICING · IP · SUPPORT | EMAIL ME ANYTIME

header.desc: Everything clients typically ask before we start — availability, pricing, payments, IP ownership, support, working style. Honest answers, no marketing fluff. If something's missing, send me a note.

items.q: What's your current availability?
items.a: I accept a maximum of 3 concurrent projects to protect quality. Typical project start is 1–2 weeks after contract signing. For urgent needs I offer a rush option with a 20% premium.

items.q: Do you work with international clients?
items.a: Yes. I'm based in Dubai but most of my freelance work is remote. I regularly collaborate with clients across Europe, the US, and MENA. Daily standup in your timezone is not a problem.

items.q: Do you work alone or with a team?
items.a: For small-to-medium projects (under $10k): solo. For larger enterprise engagements I lead a hand-picked team of 2–5 trusted engineers under my direct supervision. You always deal with me as the primary contact.

items.q: What if I only have a rough idea, not a spec?
items.a: That's the norm, not the exception. The discovery call and proposal stage exist exactly for that — we turn your rough idea into a scoped, estimated, realistic plan. No need to write a spec to get started.

items.q: Can you take over a project someone else started?
items.a: Yes, but I require a paid technical audit first ($1,200, fixed). This protects us both — I can see what I'm inheriting, and you get a written report either way, even if we decide not to continue together.

groups.desc: How engagements are priced and how money moves.

items.q: What's your payment structure?
items.a: Standard: 30% upfront, 40% at mid-project milestone, 30% upon delivery. Accepted methods: bank transfer (AED, USD, EUR), Wise, PayPal, or crypto (USDT). All fees are fixed — no hidden surprises.

items.q: Do you offer hourly rates?
items.a: For ad-hoc work, yes — $75/hour. For full projects, I strongly prefer fixed pricing. Fixed pricing aligns incentives: I'm paid to deliver outcomes, not to spend hours.

items.q: What happens if scope changes mid-project?
items.a: Change requests get a written mini-proposal: what it is, how it impacts timeline, and a fixed price add-on. You approve or decline before I touch a line of code. No silent scope creep.

items.q: Do you invoice in AED, USD, or EUR?
items.a: All three. Invoices are issued from my Dubai entity with proper VAT handling. I can also invoice in GBP on request.

items.q: Are there any discounts?
items.a: Nonprofits and early-stage founders (pre-seed, no funding) can ask for a 15% scope discount. I don't negotiate pricing otherwise — my rates are already set fairly for the deliverable.

groups.desc: What you get, and what happens after launch.

items.q: What if I need changes after delivery?
items.a: Every project comes with 30 days of free bug fixes. For new features or major changes, monthly maintenance retainers start at $800/month. Hourly rate $75/hour for ad-hoc work.

items.q: Where does the code get deployed?
items.a: On your infrastructure, always. Your cloud, your servers, your domain. I don't host client code. You own the deployment pipeline and credentials from day one.

items.q: What if you become unavailable mid-project?
items.a: Every project has a written continuity plan — repository access, deployment runbook, and architecture doc. If something happens to me, any competent Laravel/React engineer can pick up where I left off. No lock-in.

items.q: Do you provide documentation?
items.a: Yes — architecture overview, API reference (OpenAPI spec), a deployment runbook, and a short handover video. All included. Documentation is a deliverable, not an afterthought.

items.q: How fast do you respond during a project?
items.a: Average response time during active work: under 4 hours in business hours (GST/GMT+4). For urgent production issues on clients with retainers, under 1 hour.

groups.desc: Who owns what, and how we protect it.

items.q: How do you handle intellectual property?
items.a: I sign NDAs before any detailed discussion — free of charge. All code, files, and documentation belong to you 100%. I don't use your project in my portfolio without explicit written permission.

items.q: Do you use open-source or proprietary libraries?
items.a: I use permissively-licensed open-source libraries (MIT, Apache, BSD). No AGPL, no copyleft surprises, no commercial SDKs without your explicit approval. Every dependency is audited before shipping.

items.q: What contract do you work under?
items.a: Either my standard MSA + SOW, or yours — I'm happy to review and sign reasonable client agreements. Dubai jurisdiction by default, but I've worked under English, EU and US law as well.

items.q: Can I see examples of past NDAs or contracts?
items.a: Templates yes, signed ones no — that would defeat the point of the NDA. Ask and I'll send you my current MSA and mutual NDA templates for your legal team to review.

items.q: Do you carry professional insurance?
items.a: Yes — professional indemnity coverage via a Dubai-based insurer. Certificate of coverage available on request during contracting.

help.desc: No problem. Send me the question directly — I'll reply within a few hours, and I won't charge you to ask.

contactPage.metaDesc: Get in touch — email, WhatsApp, LinkedIn, or book a free 30-minute discovery call. Replies in under 4 hours, GMT+4.
contactPage.ticker: CONTACT · DUBAI · GMT+4 | RESPONSE UNDER 4H IN BUSINESS HOURS | FREE NDA · FREE DISCOVERY CALL

intro.desc: Tell me what you're trying to ship. I reply inside 4 hours in business hours (Dubai, GMT+4), often much faster. No obligation, no sales pitch — if we're not the right fit I'll say so and point you somewhere better.

sla.headline: 4-hour average response
sla.desc: Business hours, GMT+4. Under 1 hour for retainer clients on urgent issues.

where.desc: Based in Dubai (GMT+4). Most engagements are remote, but I meet local clients in person when it helps. Regularly work across MENA, Europe, and the US with live overlap hours.
where.weekend: Weekend response · within 24h for urgent items

items.q: How fast do you respond?
items.a: Average is under 4 hours during business hours (GMT+4). Urgent items on retainer clients are under an hour.

items.q: Do I need a finished spec to reach out?
items.a: No. Most clients start with a rough idea. The discovery call exists to turn it into a scoped, estimated, realistic plan.

items.q: Can you sign an NDA before we talk details?
items.a: Yes, and it's free. Send me yours or use my mutual template. We sign before anything sensitive hits a message.

items.q: Can we meet in person if I'm in Dubai?
items.a: Happy to — DIFC, Business Bay or Downtown. Just propose a coffee spot in the form or on WhatsApp and I'll confirm.
